title: Doing something to every item
body: A *loop* repeats work. `for x in things:` runs its block once for each item in `things`. `range(1, n+1)` gives you the numbers 1..n to loop over. The most common pattern is the **accumulator**: start a total at 0 (or a list at []), then update it each time through the loop. That one pattern — start empty, loop, build up — is behind a huge amount of code. The next two challenges drill it.
